## engineering.coding.explicit_diagnosable_flow - Explicit Diagnosable Flow

- document: `context-docs/ENGINEERING.json`
- type: `rule`
- priority: `should`
- subtype: `coding_rule`
- load_at_start: `true`
- tags: `engineering`, `coding`, `architecture`, `diagnostics`
- source_of_truth: `context-docs/ENGINEERING.json`

Prefer explicit data flow, ownership, boundaries, and verification over hidden or clever behavior.

Keep implementation easy to trace mechanically: clear entry points, explicit state changes, source-of-truth ownership, boundary normalization, and verification attached to risky behavior.

Rationale: SocratexPipeline is meant to preserve long-running agent continuity; implicit systems make that continuity fragile.

Sources:
- `core/AGENT-CONTRACT.json` `operating_principles` - Consolidated from the shared agent contract.
- `project/code/WORKFLOW.json` `general_workflow` duplicate - Code-project workflow repeats the same future-fit and diagnostic bias.
name: engineering.coding.explicit_diagnosable_flow
summary: Prefer explicit data flow, ownership, boundaries, and verification over hidden or clever behavior.
title: Explicit Diagnosable Flow

## engineering.workflow.context_tags_before_response - Context Tags Before Response

- document: `context-docs/ENGINEERING.json`
- type: `rule`
- priority: `must`
- subtype: `workflow_rule`
- load_at_start: `true`
- tags: `engineering`, `workflow`, `docs-workflow`, `compiled-context`, `knowledge`
- source_of_truth: `context-docs/ENGINEERING.json`

Commands and substantive questions should route through main workflow context, prompt-derived tags, and compiled notes before answer or execution.

For every user command or substantive question, load the main workflow/instruction context first, derive context tags from the user text with `tools/knowledge/context_tags.ps1` when available, then query compiled notes with `tools/knowledge/knowledge_select.ps1 -Tags <tags> -Match any` before answering or executing. Use tag-selected notes as routing and risk context; read exact source documents before edits, high-stakes claims, or ambiguity resolution.

Rationale: Prompt-derived tags make compiled knowledge useful beyond broad startup slices and reduce missed workflow, architecture, diagnostic, or documentation constraints.

Sources:
- `core/AGENT-CONTRACT.json` `operating_principles` - Shared contract requires tag-derived context before substantive answers and commands.
- `project/code/WORKFLOW.json` `read_order` duplicate - Code workflow defines the executable context-tag pass.
- `tools/knowledge/context_tags.ps1` `script` duplicate - Source pipeline helper for deriving context tags from user text.
name: engineering.workflow.context_tags_before_response
summary: Commands and substantive questions should route through main workflow context, prompt-derived tags, and compiled notes before answer or execution.
title: Context Tags Before Response

## engineering.workflow.default_tool_error_hardening - Default Tool Error Hardening

- document: `context-docs/ENGINEERING.json`
- type: `rule`
- priority: `must`
- subtype: `workflow_rule`
- load_at_start: `true`
- tags: `engineering`, `workflow`, `tooling`, `automation`, `scripts`, `docs-workflow`, `verification`
- source_of_truth: `context-docs/ENGINEERING.json`

Tool invocation failures should default to hardening the owning script or contract, not repeating manual recovery.

When an owned repository script or tool fails because an input was easy to pass incorrectly, quoting/encoding/path handling was fragile, validation was missing, output was unclear, or a process failure is likely to recur, default to fixing or extending the owning script, wrapper, help/validation, or SCRIPTS.json input/output contract before retrying or immediately after unblocking the task. Treat manual recovery as temporary; keep it only when the failure is clearly a one-off typo, missing user-provided data, or the tool-hardening scope would be materially larger than the current task.

Rationale: Repeatable tool friction is pipeline debt. Encoding the fix into scripts and script contracts reduces future prompt cost and prevents agents from memorizing brittle workarounds.

Sources:
- `WORKFLOW.json` `tool_error_hardening` - Source workflow rule makes tool hardening the default response to repeatable tool-result errors.
- `SCRIPTS.json` `metadata.script_io_contract` duplicate - Script catalog can carry exact input/output contracts for fragile invocation shapes.
name: engineering.workflow.default_tool_error_hardening
summary: Tool invocation failures should default to hardening the owning script or contract, not repeating manual recovery.
title: Default Tool Error Hardening

## engineering.workflow.knowledge_file_fallback - Use File Knowledge Fallback When SQLite Is Unavailable

- document: `context-docs/ENGINEERING.json`
- type: `rule`
- priority: `should`
- subtype: `workflow_rule`
- load_at_start: `true`
- tags: `engineering`, `workflow`, `docs-workflow`, `verification`
- source_of_truth: `context-docs/ENGINEERING.json`

The JSON table fallback mirrors the core SQLite table model but intentionally omits named views.

If SQLite cannot be used, read compiled knowledge from `AI-compiled/project/knowledge-files/` with `tools/knowledge/knowledge_file_select.ps1`; query by tags, type, source path, document path, entry name, or startup flag instead of named views.

Rationale: Some hosts may not allow SQLite writes or may lock the database file, but agents still need a compact compiled knowledge layer.

Sources:
- `context-docs/ENGINEERING.json` `tagged_rules` - Source pipeline file-fallback rule.
name: engineering.workflow.knowledge_file_fallback
summary: The JSON table fallback mirrors the core SQLite table model but intentionally omits named views.
title: Use File Knowledge Fallback When SQLite Is Unavailable

## engineering.workflow.knowledge_hash_gate - Validate Source Hashes Before Trusting SQLite

- document: `context-docs/ENGINEERING.json`
- type: `rule`
- priority: `must`
- subtype: `verification_rule`
- load_at_start: `true`
- tags: `engineering`, `verification`, `docs-workflow`
- source_of_truth: `context-docs/ENGINEERING.json`

A compiled knowledge database is trustworthy only when its tracked source hashes match current documents.

Run `tools/knowledge/knowledge_check.ps1` before relying on existing compiled knowledge; if stale documents are reported, refresh them with `tools/knowledge/knowledge_upsert.ps1 -Path <document>` or rebuild with `tools/knowledge/knowledge_compile.ps1`.

Rationale: Hash checks prevent agents from reading stale compiled knowledge after source documents change.

Sources:
- `context-docs/ENGINEERING.json` `tagged_rules` - Source pipeline starter rule.
name: engineering.workflow.knowledge_hash_gate
summary: A compiled knowledge database is trustworthy only when its tracked source hashes match current documents.
title: Validate Source Hashes Before Trusting SQLite

## engineering.workflow.knowledge_sqlite_generated_layer - SQLite Knowledge Is Generated

- document: `context-docs/ENGINEERING.json`
- type: `rule`
- priority: `must`
- subtype: `workflow_rule`
- load_at_start: `true`
- tags: `engineering`, `workflow`, `docs-workflow`, `verification`
- source_of_truth: `context-docs/ENGINEERING.json`

The SQLite knowledge database is a compiled index, not a manually edited source of truth.

Edit source documents first, then refresh `AI-compiled/project/knowledge.sqlite` with `tools/knowledge/knowledge_compile.ps1` for a full rebuild or `tools/knowledge/knowledge_upsert.ps1 -Path <document>` for a targeted document update.

Rationale: This preserves readable source documents while giving agents fast tag, type, source, and startup-context queries.

Sources:
- `context-docs/ENGINEERING.json` `tagged_rules` - Source pipeline starter rule.
name: engineering.workflow.knowledge_sqlite_generated_layer
summary: The SQLite knowledge database is a compiled index, not a manually edited source of truth.
title: SQLite Knowledge Is Generated

## engineering.workflow.knowledge_views - Query Context Through Named Views

- document: `context-docs/ENGINEERING.json`
- type: `rule`
- priority: `should`
- subtype: `workflow_rule`
- load_at_start: `true`
- tags: `engineering`, `workflow`, `docs-workflow`
- source_of_truth: `context-docs/ENGINEERING.json`

Use named knowledge views for common context lanes before broader document reads.

Prefer `tools/knowledge/knowledge_select.ps1 -View <view_id>` for prepared context lanes such as `session_start`, `debugging`, `performance`, and `docs_workflow`; use tag/type/source queries when the task needs a narrower slice.

Rationale: Named views reduce context cost and make repeated agent startup/query behavior consistent across projects.

Sources:
- `context-docs/ENGINEERING.json` `tagged_rules` - Source pipeline starter rule.
name: engineering.workflow.knowledge_views
summary: Use named knowledge views for common context lanes before broader document reads.
title: Query Context Through Named Views

## engineering.workflow.load_standards_before_code - Load Engineering Standards Before Code Work

- document: `context-docs/ENGINEERING.json`
- type: `rule`
- priority: `must`
- subtype: `workflow_rule`
- load_at_start: `true`
- tags: `engineering`, `workflow`, `coding`, `architecture`, `best-practices`, `borrowed-before-bespoke`, `production-grade`, `ddd-adiv`, `future-first`, `docs-workflow`
- source_of_truth: `context-docs/ENGINEERING.json`

Any code-touching task must load the full compiled code-guidance set before source edits.

For implementation, refactor, bugfix, code review, or any task that may touch source code, run `tools/knowledge/knowledge_code_context.ps1` before the first code read/write that could lead to an edit. The loaded base set must include engineering, coding, architecture, best practices, borrowed-before-bespoke, production-grade software engineering, DDD-ADIV, future-first, data-first ownership, runtime boundaries, diagnostics, performance, and verification rules. Additional task views may be added, but they must not replace the full base set. `tools/repo/check_task.ps1` should fail changed-code checks when the fresh `ignored/code_context_gate.json` marker from that helper is missing, loaded for another HEAD, or incomplete.

Rationale: Professional code quality depends on keeping the complete active project engineering standard in context every time, not on a narrow subset or visible-chat memory.

Sources:
- `project/code/WORKFLOW.json` `read_order` - Code workflow read-order requirement for programming tasks.
- `project/code/WORKFLOW.json` `script_first_execution` duplicate - Script-first workflow repeats the preferred query command.
- `tools/knowledge/knowledge_code_context.ps1` `script` duplicate - Canonical helper for loading the code-task engineering context slice.
- `tools/codebase/check_code_context_gate.ps1` `script` duplicate - Verification guard for changed-code tasks.
name: engineering.workflow.load_standards_before_code
summary: Any code-touching task must load the full compiled code-guidance set before source edits.
title: Load Engineering Standards Before Code Work

compiled_content:
## engineering.workflow.normalized_content_hashes - Normalized Content Hashes For Compiled Knowledge

- document: `context-docs/ENGINEERING.json`
- type: `rule`
- priority: `must`
- subtype: `workflow_rule`
- load_at_start: `true`
- tags: `engineering`, `workflow`, `tooling`, `compiled-context`, `knowledge`, `hashing`
- source_of_truth: `context-docs/ENGINEERING.json`

Compiled knowledge and instruction freshness hashes ignore mechanical text drift but preserve semantic whitespace.

Use normalized text-content hashes for compiled knowledge, file fallback tables, compiled instruction checksums, and generated instruction source manifests: remove UTF-8 BOM, fold CRLF/CR to LF, trim trailing spaces and tabs per line, and ignore excess final blank lines. Do not use file modification timestamps for freshness, and do not strip all whitespace, because those approaches respectively create false invalidations and hide semantic changes in JSON, Python, PowerShell, and other whitespace-sensitive formats.

Rationale: Compiled outputs should rebuild when source meaning changes, not when editor or line-ending cleanup changes; preserving indentation and interior whitespace keeps the hash safe for whitespace-sensitive files.

Sources:
- `tools/knowledge/knowledge_index.py` `normalized_hash_text` - Knowledge SQLite and file fallback source freshness use the normalized hash policy.
- `tools/pipeline/rebuild_ai_compiled_context.ps1` `ConvertTo-NormalizedHashText` duplicate - Compiled instruction source manifests and checksums use the same normalized hash policy.
name: engineering.workflow.normalized_content_hashes
summary: Compiled knowledge and instruction freshness hashes ignore mechanical text drift but preserve semantic whitespace.
title: Normalized Content Hashes For Compiled Knowledge
